original: Elon Musklaunches Tesla's electric Cybertruck with shatterproof windows that when demonstrated on stage shatter
Authorities at the British Natural History Museum announce the "Piltdown Man" skull, one of the most famous fossil skulls in the world, is a hoax
Evidence presented of the earliest known alphabet, on clay cylinders, 4,400 years old, 500 years older than previous writing, discovered in tombs in Umm el-Marra, Syria
Jean-François Pilâtre de Rozier and Marquis d'Arlandes make 1st manned free balloon flight in a Montgolfier balloon
Thomas Morris of Australia skips rope 22,806 times
Phillip & Jay Kunz fly a kite a record 28,000 feet
Edison lab records the 1st surviving motion picture, "Monkeyshines No. 1", shot by William am Kennedy Dickson and William Heise [date disputed between June 1889 and November 21–27, 1890]
Verrazano-Narrows suspension bridge opens in New York City, then the world's longest
Christopher Tolkien, British author and son of J. R. R. Tolkien, born in Leeds, England (d. 2020)
René Magritte, Belgian surrealist painter (This is Not a Pipe), born in Lessines, Belgium (d. 1967)
Robert Brown, Scottish botanist (discovered 'Brownian Motion'), born in Montrose, Scotland (d. 1858)
Harry Truman becomes 1st US President to travel in a submerged sub
India celebrates 50th anniversary of IR8, rice variety specially bred that saved much of Asia from famine
Mt Agung on the Indonesian island of Bali begins erupting
Adam Ondracompletes the second free ascent of The Dawn Wall at El Capitan, leading every pitch in 8 days
Victor Chang, Australian pioneering cardiac surgeon, born in Shanghai, China (d. 1986)
NY Rangers scores a NHL record 8 goals in 1 period
1st night football game, Philadelphia Athletics beats Kanaweola AC, 39-0
"Rocky" directed by John G. Avildsen and starringSylvester Stallonepremieres in New York (Best Picture 1977)
"Happy" single is released byPharrell Williams(Billboard Song of the Year 2014, Grammy Award for Best Pop Solo Performance 2015)

Troy Aikman, American College/Pro Football HOF quarterback (University of Oklahoma, UCLA; Super Bowl 1992 [MVP], 93, 95; 6 x Pro Bowl; Dallas Cowboys) and broadcaster (Fox, ESPN MNF), born in West Covina, California
Marlo Thomas, American actress (That Girl; Jenny), and writer (Free to Be... You and Me), born in Detroit Michigan
Colleen Ballinger, American YouTuber and comedian (Miranda Sings), born in Santa Barbara, California
Ronny Chieng, Malaysian comedian and actor known for "The Daily Show", born in Johor Bahru, Malaysia
Brie Bella, American pro wrestler, one half of The Bella Twins and reality TV star (Total Divas), born in San Diego, California
Jan Karlsson swims world record 50m butterfly (23.80 sec)
Jani Sievinen swims world record 100m medley (53.78 sec)
Louise Karlsson swims world record 50m freestyle (31.19 sec)
Alberto Juantorena, Cuban athlete (Olympic gold 400/800m 1976; WR 800m 1:43.44), born in Santiago, Cuba
Earl "the Pearl" Monroe, American basketball HOF guard (4 x NBA All-Star; NBA C'ship 1973 NY Knicks; NBA Rookie of the Year 1968 Baltimore Bullets), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania
Harold Ramis, American actor and director (SCTV, Stripes), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2014)
Eleanor Powell, American stage and screen actress and tap dancer (Born to Dance, Broadway Melody films), born in Springfield, Massachusetts (d. 1982)
Coleman Hawkins, American jazz musician who virtually created tenor saxophone for jazz, born in St. Joseph, Missouri (d. 1969)
Cherie Johnson, American actress (Punky Brewster - "Cherie"), born in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania
NY Yankees buyJoe DiMaggiofrom SF Seals (Pacific Coast League)
EntertainerJack Benny(violin) and US Vice-PresidentRichard Nixon(piano) play their famed duet
Roger Federerof Switzerland wins back-to-back season-ending Tennis Masters Cup titles with a 6–3, 6–2 victory over Australian Leyton Hewitt in the final in Houston, Texas
Alexander Zverevof Germany captures his second ATP Finals men's tennis title defeating world #2Daniil Medvedev6-4, 6-4 in Turin
British driverLewis Hamiltonwins 1st Qatar Grand Prix Formula One motor race at the Losail International Circuit in Qatar
MLB Los Angeles DodgersShohei Ohtani(NL) and New York YankeesAaron Judge(AL) both unanimously chosen as winners of their league MVP
Pete Rose(Cincinnati Reds) wins MLB's NL MVP
MLB Boston Red Sox Carlton Fisk wins AL Rookie of Year, New York Mets Jon Matlack wins NL

Sid Luckman, American College-Pro Football HOF quarterback (Columbia University; 4 × NFL C'ship 1940, 41, 43, 46; NFL MVP 1943; 5 × First-team All-Pro; 3 × Pro Bowl; Chicago Bears), born in Brooklyn, New York (d. 1998)
Sabine Busch, German athlete (World C'ship gold GDR 4 × 400m relay 1983, 87; 400m hurdles 1987), born in Erfurt, Germany
Toshihiko Koga, Japanese judoka (Olympic gold - 71kgs 1992; silver 1996), born in Kitashigeyasu, Saga, Japan (d. 2021)
Henry Hartsfield Jr, American astronaut (STS-4; STS-41-D; STS-61-A), born in Birmingham, Alabama (d. 2014)
RIBA’s best building in the world biennial international prize awarded to Canuanã school in Brazil
"Voiceless Mass" by Raven Chacon, premieres at the Cathedral of St. John the Evangelist in Milwaukee - first work by an Native American to win the Pulitzer Prize for Music
US PresidentJohn F. Kennedyflies to Texas (assassinated the next day)
The Alabama parole board grants posthumous pardons to three members of the Scottsboro boys
Competing explorersHenry Morton Stanley, in the service of Belgian KingLeopold IIand Italian-French explorerPierre Savorgnan de Brazza, working for France, meet in the lower Congo
Dmitri Shostakovich's 5th Symphony premieres in Lenningrad, with the ovation lasting over an hour
"All 'N All" 8th studio album by Earth, Wind & Fire is released (Billboard Album of the Year 1978)
The Supremes& The Temptations release "I'm Gonna Make You Love Me"
Olivia Newton-John's "Physical" single goes #1 & stays for 10 weeks
German tennis star Michael Stich scores a 7–6, 2–6, 7–6, 6–2 win over AmericanPete Samprasto win his first and only ATP Tour World Championship title in Frankfurt, Germany
Bill Longson beatsLou Theszin St Louis, to win National Wrestling Association World Heavyweight title
Baseball's Philadelphia Athletics & Phillies form pro football teams, joining Pitts Stars in 1st attempt at a National Football League
Island of Dominica hit by the most destructive earthquake in its history. Damage concentrated in the north and the town of Portsmouth. Also felt in neighboring Guadeloupe, where one person is killed.
WHO says Measles has killed more than 5,000 people in Democratic Republic of Congo in 2019, in world's largest and fastest-moving epidemic
